title: Add collapsible sections in Details panel
description: Refactor the Details panel to use Textual collapsible UI sections for long attributes (at minimum Acceptance Criteria, Handoff, and File lists) so operators can fold/unfold content. Use Textual's Collapsible widget (or the equivalent built-in collapsible control) rather than manual string-only rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Details panel supports fold/unfold for long sections and defaults to a readable compact state.
Implementation uses a proper Textual collapsible widget/control (Collapsible or equivalent built-in).
Keyboard and mouse interaction for expand/collapse is documented and regression-tested.

handoff_summary.completed: Refactored the Textual detail panel in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` to render a summary block plus Collapsible sections for long metadata. Added active-section tracking with `[` / `]` navigation, `Enter` toggle behavior in detail focus, section state syncing for mouse clicks, and updated help/subtitle text. Extended `tests/test_tui.py` with regressions for compact defaults, keyboard toggling, mouse header toggling, and adjusted existing detail-panel render assertions. Documented the new detail-section controls and default collapsed behavior in `README.md`.
handoff_summary.risks: Detail scrolling still uses the existing runtime scroll-offset model, so the visual scroll behavior remains tied to the prior panel-level mechanics while section visibility is now dynamic. Current regression coverage passed for the shipped flows.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: This bead edits the shared TUI implementation, its regression suite, and the operator README; downstream agents touching the same panel behavior or shortcut docs should rebase carefully on these files.

conflict_risks: This bead edits the shared TUI implementation, its regression suite, and the operator README; downstream agents touching the same panel behavior or shortcut docs should rebase carefully on these files.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
